381		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
382		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
383		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
384		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
385		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
386		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
387		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
388		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
389		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
390		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours

394	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
395	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
396	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
397	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
398	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
399		if configured == "" {
400			configured = DefaultLogDir
401		}
402		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
403			return configured
404		}
405		if projectRoot == "" {
406			return configured
407		}
408		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
409	}

117	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
118	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
119	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
120	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
121	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
122	// worktree.
123	type GitOps interface {
124		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
125		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
126		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
127		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
128		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
129		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
130		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
131		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
132		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
133		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
134		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
135		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
136		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
137		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
138		// other work that might prune it.
139		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
140		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
141		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
142		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
143	}

235	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
236	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
237	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
238		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")
239		if base == "" {
240			base = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)
241		}
242		return filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+"-"+attemptID)
243	}

315	func dirtyWorktreePaths(dir string) []string {
316		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "status", "--porcelain", "--untracked-files=all").Output()
317		if err != nil {
318			return nil
319		}
320		var paths []string
321		seen := map[string]bool{}
322		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
323			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
324			if line == "" || len(line) < 4 {
325				continue
326			}
327			path := strings.TrimSpace(line[3:])
328			if idx := strings.Index(path, " -> "); idx >= 0 {
329				path = strings.TrimSpace(path[idx+4:])
330			}
331			if path == "" || seen[path] {
332				continue
333			}
334			seen[path] = true
335			paths = append(paths, path)
336		}
337		return paths
338	}

340	// EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
341	// applied when landEvidence / VerifyCleanWorktree stage the evidence
342	// directory. Only excludes the multi-thousand-line embedded session log
343	// (the single file that actually explodes past provider context
344	// limits). prompt.md, usage.json, manifest.json, and result.json remain
345	// tracked — they are small and serve the audit-trail contract. The
346	// gitignore written by `ddx init` already treats .ddx/executions/*/
347	// embedded as ignored; this pathspec is defence-in-depth for force-add
348	// paths.
349	//
350	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896 — session logs landed as tracked
351	// evidence caused retry prompts to balloon past 2M+ tokens and crash
352	// every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
353	func EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs() []string {
354		return []string{
355			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
356		}
357	}
358	
359	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs returns git pathspec-exclusion fragments
360	// applied when review-prompt synthesis runs `git show <rev>` over the
361	// evidence commit. Broader than EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs: excludes
362	// prompt.md and usage.json too, because even though they're tracked for
363	// audit, they're execution-artifact noise from the reviewer's
364	// perspective — the reviewer wants to see the implementation diff, not
365	// the prior attempt's prompt or token counters. This also protects
366	// against old commits (pre-fix) that committed the session log
367	// directly.
368	//
369	// Regression anchor: ddx-39e27896.
370	func EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs() []string {
371		return []string{
372			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/embedded/**",
373			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md",
374			":(exclude,glob).ddx/executions/*/usage.json",
375		}
376	}

378	// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise
379	// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)
380	// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit
381	// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.
382	func (r *RealGitOps) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {
383		// Do NOT list already-gitignored paths (.ddx/agent-logs, .ddx/workers) as
384		// :(exclude) pathspecs. Git treats a path named by :(exclude) as explicitly
385		// referenced, so when the path is also .gitignored git emits "The following
386		// paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files" AND exits 1 — even
387		// though the pathspec is trying to SKIP it. Paths already in .gitignore are
388		// excluded by default; excludes here are only for paths that would
389		// otherwise be tracked.
390		addArgs := []string{
391			"add", "-A", "--",
392			".",
393		}
394		addArgs = append(addArgs, synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir)...)
395		if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, addArgs...).Run(); err != nil {
396			return false, fmt.Errorf("staging changes: %w", err)
397		}
398		statusOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only").Output()
399		if len(bytes.TrimSpace(statusOut)) == 0 {
400			return false, nil
401		}
402		if msg == "" {
403			msg = "chore: execute-bead synthesized result commit"
404		}
405		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "commit", "-m", msg).CombinedOutput()
406		if err != nil {
407			return false, fmt.Errorf("synthesize commit: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
408		}
409		return true, nil
410	}
411	
412	func synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs(dir string) []string {
413		candidates := []struct {
414			pathspec    string
415			ignoreProbe string
416		}{
417			{
418				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded",
419				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/embedded",
420			},
421			{
422				pathspec:    ":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt",
423				ignoreProbe: ".ddx/executions/.ddx-check-ignore/no_changes_rationale.txt",
424			},
425			{
426				pathspec:    ":(exclude).claude/skills",
427				ignoreProbe: ".claude/skills",
428			},
429			{
430				pathspec:    ":(exclude).agents/skills",
431				ignoreProbe: ".agents/skills",
432			},
433		}
434	
435		pathspecs := make([]string, 0, len(candidates))
436		for _, c := range candidates {
437			if isGitIgnored(dir, c.ignoreProbe) {
438				continue
439			}
440			pathspecs = append(pathspecs, c.pathspec)
441		}
442		return pathspecs
443	}

446		err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "check-ignore", "-q", "--", path).Run()
447		return err == nil
448	}

464	// appendBeadRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence entry on the bead
465	// after an agent run. It is a best-effort operation — errors are silently
466	// ignored so a store failure never aborts the main execute-bead flow.
467	//
468	// The body separates requested passthrough constraints (harness/provider/model
469	// from the CLI envelope) and requested power bounds from the actual resolved
470	// values so analytics can distinguish intent from outcome (AC5 of ddx-20047dd5).
471	func appendBeadRoutingEvidence(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, harness, provider, model, routeReason, baseURL string, passthrough config.AgentPassthrough, minPower, maxPower int) {
472		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
473			return
474		}
475		resolvedProvider := provider
476		if resolvedProvider == "" {
477			resolvedProvider = harness
478		}
479		type routingBody struct {
480			ResolvedProvider  string   `json:"resolved_provider"`
481			ResolvedModel     string   `json:"resolved_model,omitempty"`
482			RouteReason       string   `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
483			FallbackChain     []string `json:"fallback_chain"`
484			BaseURL           string   `json:"base_url,omitempty"`
485			RequestedHarness  string   `json:"requested_harness,omitempty"`
486			RequestedProvider string   `json:"requested_provider,omitempty"`
487			RequestedModel    string   `json:"requested_model,omitempty"`
488			RequestedMinPower int      `json:"requested_min_power,omitempty"`
489			RequestedMaxPower int      `json:"requested_max_power,omitempty"`
490		}
491		body := routingBody{
492			ResolvedProvider:  resolvedProvider,
493			ResolvedModel:     model,
494			RouteReason:       routeReason,
495			FallbackChain:     []string{},
496			BaseURL:           baseURL,
497			RequestedHarness:  passthrough.Harness,
498			RequestedProvider: passthrough.Provider,
499			RequestedModel:    passthrough.Model,
500			RequestedMinPower: minPower,
501			RequestedMaxPower: maxPower,
502		}
503		data, err := json.Marshal(body)
504		if err != nil {
505			return
506		}
507		summary := fmt.Sprintf("provider=%s", resolvedProvider)
508		if model != "" {
509			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" model=%s", model)
510		}
511		if routeReason != "" {
512			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" reason=%s", routeReason)
513		}
514		_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
515			Kind:    "routing",
516			Summary: summary,
517			Body:    string(data),
518			Actor:   "ddx",
519			Source:  "ddx agent execute-bead",
520		})
521	}

533		TotalTokens  int     `json:"total_tokens"`
534		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
535		DurationMS   int     `json:"duration_ms"`
536		ExitCode     int     `json:"exit_code"`
537	}

539	// appendBeadCostEvidence records a kind:cost evidence entry on the bead with
540	// per-attempt token and dollar usage. Best-effort: errors are discarded so a
541	// store failure never aborts the main execute-bead flow. Emits nothing when
542	// the appender is nil, the beadID is empty, or every cost field is zero
543	// (e.g., dry-run, no-changes with no provider call).
544	func appendBeadCostEvidence(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, attemptID string, body costEventBody) {
545		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
546			return
547		}
548		if body.InputTokens == 0 && body.OutputTokens == 0 && body.TotalTokens == 0 && body.CostUSD == 0 {
549			return
550		}
551		body.AttemptID = attemptID
552		if body.TotalTokens == 0 {
553			body.TotalTokens = body.InputTokens + body.OutputTokens
554		}
555		data, err := json.Marshal(body)
556		if err != nil {
557			return
558		}
559		var summary string
560		if body.CostUSD > 0 {
561			summary = fmt.Sprintf("tokens=%d cost_usd=%.4f", body.TotalTokens, body.CostUSD)
562		} else {
563			summary = fmt.Sprintf("tokens=%d", body.TotalTokens)
564		}
565		if body.Model != "" {
566			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" model=%s", body.Model)
567		}
568		_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
569			Kind:    "cost",
570			Summary: summary,
571			Body:    string(data),
572			Actor:   "ddx",
573			Source:  "ddx agent execute-bead",
574		})
575	}

577	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the thin worker: it creates an isolated worktree,
578	// constructs the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness,
579	// synthesizes a commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up
580	// the worktree and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one
581	// of:
582	//
583	//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits
584	//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero
585	//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0, made no commits, and wrote a rationale
586	//   - task_no_evidence: agent exited 0 but made no commits and wrote no rationale
587	//
588	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
589	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
590	//
591	// Agent dispatch: production callers leave runtime.Service and runtime.AgentRunner
592	// nil. ExecuteBeadWithConfig constructs a fresh agentlib.DdxAgent from
593	// projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir and dispatches via RunViaServiceWith.
594	// Tests may set runtime.AgentRunner to inject a fake that returns canned
595	// Result values; when set, it takes precedence over the service path.
596	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
597		if ctx == nil {
598			ctx = context.Background()
599		}
600		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
601		if runtime.WorkerID == "" {
602			runtime.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
603		}
604	
605		wtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID)
606		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {
607			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w", mkErr)
608		}
609	
610		// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot
611		// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
612		if err := CommitTracker(projectRoot); err != nil {
613			return nil, err
614		}
615	
616		// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
617		// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). The new HEAD becomes the effective
618		// base for the worker worktree; caller's edits are preserved as a normal
619		// commit they can `git reset HEAD~` if they want them back uncommitted.
620		if _, err := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead "+attemptID); err != nil {
621			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
622		}
623	
624		// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
625		baseRev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, runtime.FromRev)
626		if err != nil {
627			return nil, err
628		}
629	
630		// Create the isolated worktree. Orphan recovery is the parent's responsibility
631		// (call RecoverOrphans before invoking workers).
632		if err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {
633			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating isolated worktree: %w", err)
634		}
635		defer func() {
636			_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wtPath)
637		}()
638	
639		// Publish the live run-state so operators and HELIX can observe what is
640		// executing without polling the bead tracker (CONTRACT-001 §5). The file
641		// is removed on completion; a crashed worker leaves a stale file that
642		// RecoverOrphans sweeps before the next attempt.
643		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
644			BeadID:       beadID,
645			AttemptID:    attemptID,
646			Harness:      rcfg.Harness(),
647			Model:        rcfg.Model(),
648			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
649			WorktreePath: wtPath,
650		})
651		defer func() {
652			_ = ClearRunState(projectRoot)
653		}()
654	
655		// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside
656		// the freshly created worktree.
657		_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)
658	
659		// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).
660		artifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, rcfg, runtime)
661		if err != nil {
662			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
663				BeadID:    beadID,
664				AttemptID: attemptID,
665				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
666				BaseRev:   baseRev,
667				ResultRev: baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output
668				ExitCode:  1,
669				Error:     err.Error(),
670				Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
671			}
672			if abInfo, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)); abInfo != nil && abInfo.IsDir() {
673				res.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
674			}
675			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
676			populateWorkerStatus(res)
677			_ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID, "result.json"), res)
678			return res, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead context load: %w", err)
679		}
680	
681		// Pre-create the execution bundle dir in the worktree so the agent can write
682		// artifacts (e.g. no_changes_rationale.txt) without needing to create the
683		// directory itself. Failures are non-fatal: the agent can create it on demand.
684		_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel), 0o755)
685	
686		// Redirect per-run session/telemetry output into the DDx-owned execution
687		// bundle so the embedded harness does not accumulate state at the worktree root.
688		embeddedStateDir := filepath.Join(artifacts.DirAbs, "embedded")
689		if err := os.MkdirAll(embeddedStateDir, 0o755); err != nil {
690			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating embedded state dir: %w", err)
691		}
692	
693		sessionID := GenerateSessionID()
694		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
695	
696		runRuntime := AgentRunRuntime{
697			PromptFile: artifacts.PromptAbs,
698			WorkDir:    wtPath,
699			Correlation: map[string]string{
700				"bead_id":     beadID,
701				"base_rev":    baseRev,
702				"attempt_id":  attemptID,
703				"session_id":  sessionID,
704				"worker_id":   runtime.WorkerID,
705				"bundle_path": artifacts.DirRel,
706				"prompt_file": artifacts.PromptRel,
707			},
708			SessionLogDirOverride: embeddedStateDir,
709			PermissionsOverride:   "unrestricted", // isolated worktree; writes must not require approval
710		}
711	
712		agentResult, agentErr := dispatchAgentRun(ctx, projectRoot, runtime.Service, runtime.AgentRunner, rcfg, runRuntime)
713		finishedAt := time.Now().UTC()
714	
715		exitCode := 0
716		tokens := 0
717		inputTokens := 0
718		outputTokens := 0
719		costUSD := 0.0
720		resultModel := rcfg.Model()
721		resultHarness := rcfg.Harness()
722		resultProvider := ""
723		agentErrMsg := ""
724		if agentResult != nil {
725			exitCode = agentResult.ExitCode
726			tokens = agentResult.Tokens
727			inputTokens = agentResult.InputTokens
728			outputTokens = agentResult.OutputTokens
729			costUSD = agentResult.CostUSD
730			if agentResult.Error != "" {
731				agentErrMsg = agentResult.Error
732			}
733			if agentResult.Model != "" {
734				resultModel = agentResult.Model
735			}
736			if agentResult.Provider != "" {
737				resultProvider = agentResult.Provider
738			}
739			if agentResult.Harness != "" {
740				resultHarness = agentResult.Harness
741			}
742		}
743		if agentErr != nil {
744			if exitCode == 0 {
745				exitCode = 1
746			}
747			agentErrMsg = agentErr.Error()
748		}
749	
750		// Capture routing evidence from the agent result. These fields are
751		// populated by RunAgent (embedded harness) and RunScript (script harness).
752		routeReason := ""
753		routeBaseURL := ""
754		if agentResult != nil {
755			routeReason = agentResult.RouteReason
756			routeBaseURL = agentResult.ResolvedBaseURL
757		}
758	
759		// Get the HEAD of the worktree after the agent ran.
760		resultRev, revErr := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath)
761		if revErr != nil {
762			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
763				BeadID:       beadID,
764				AttemptID:    attemptID,
765				WorkerID:     runtime.WorkerID,
766				BaseRev:      baseRev,
767				ResultRev:    baseRev, // no commits readable; treat as no output
768				Harness:      resultHarness,
769				Provider:     resultProvider,
770				Model:        resultModel,
771				SessionID:    sessionID,
772				DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
773				Tokens:       tokens,
774				CostUSD:      costUSD,
775				ExitCode:     1,
776				Error:        agentErrMsg,
777				Reason:       revErr.Error(), // HeadRev failure; orchestrator prefers this over Error for Reason
778				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
779				PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
780				ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
781				ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
782				StartedAt:    startedAt,
783				FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
784				Outcome:      ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
785			}
786			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
787			populateWorkerStatus(res)
788			_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)
789			return res, fmt.Errorf("failed to read worktree HEAD: %w", revErr)
790		}
791	
792		// Write usage.json when the harness reports token usage or cost.
793		// Done before SynthesizeCommit so usage data is available in the
794		// preliminary result written for commit-message sourcing.
795		var usageFileRel string
796		if tokens > 0 || costUSD > 0 {
797			usage := executeBeadUsage{
798				AttemptID:    attemptID,
799				Harness:      resultHarness,
800				Provider:     resultProvider,
801				Model:        resultModel,
802				Tokens:       tokens,
803				InputTokens:  inputTokens,
804				OutputTokens: outputTokens,
805				CostUSD:      costUSD,
806			}
807			if writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.UsageAbs, usage); writeErr == nil {
808				usageFileRel = artifacts.UsageRel
809			}
810		}
811	
812		// Synthesize a commit when the agent left tracked edits without committing.
813		// Only do so for real changes — harness noise paths are excluded. If nothing
814		// real was staged (committed is false), leave resultRev == baseRev so the
815		// outcome is classified as task_no_changes.
816		if resultRev == baseRev {
817			if isDirty, _ := gitOps.IsDirty(wtPath); isDirty {
818				// Build a preliminary result and write it to result.json before
819				// calling SynthesizeCommit. The commit message is then sourced from
820				// the tracked artifact file, satisfying the provenance contract:
821				// "commit-message metadata must be projected from tracked artifact
822				// files, never from ad hoc runtime state" (FEAT-006).
823				// The final result.json is re-written after the commit with the
824				// correct ResultRev, Outcome, and Status.
825				prelimOutcome := ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
826				if exitCode != 0 {
827					prelimOutcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
828				}
829				prelimRes := &ExecuteBeadResult{
830					BeadID:       beadID,
831					AttemptID:    attemptID,
832					WorkerID:     runtime.WorkerID,
833					BaseRev:      baseRev,
834					ResultRev:    "", // unknown until commit is made
835					Harness:      resultHarness,
836					Provider:     resultProvider,
837					Model:        resultModel,
838					SessionID:    sessionID,
839					DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
840					Tokens:       tokens,
841					CostUSD:      costUSD,
842					ExitCode:     exitCode,
843					Error:        agentErrMsg,
844					ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
845					PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
846					ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
847					ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
848					UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
849					StartedAt:    startedAt,
850					FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
851					Outcome:      prelimOutcome,
852				}
853				populateWorkerStatus(prelimRes)
854				_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, prelimRes)
855	
856				// Render the commit message from the tracked artifact file.
857				commitMsg, msgErr := BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(artifacts.ResultAbs)
858				if msgErr != nil {
859					commitMsg = "chore: execute-bead iteration " + beadID
860				}
861	
862				if committed, synthErr := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(wtPath, commitMsg); synthErr == nil && committed {
863					if newRev, _ := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath); newRev != baseRev {
864						resultRev = newRev
865					}
866				}
867			}
868		}
869	
870		res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
871			BeadID:       beadID,
872			AttemptID:    attemptID,
873			WorkerID:     runtime.WorkerID,
874			BaseRev:      baseRev,
875			ResultRev:    resultRev,
876			Harness:      resultHarness,
877			Provider:     resultProvider,
878			Model:        resultModel,
879			SessionID:    sessionID,
880			DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
881			Tokens:       tokens,
882			CostUSD:      costUSD,
883			ExitCode:     exitCode,
884			Error:        agentErrMsg,
885			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
886			PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
887			ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
888			ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
889			UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
890			StartedAt:    startedAt,
891			FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
892		}
893	
894		// Classify worker outcome: task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes /
895		// task_no_evidence. A clean agent exit with no commit is only a legitimate
896		// no_changes signal when the agent also wrote the explicit rationale file.
897		// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) will
898		// overwrite Outcome and Status with the landing decision before output.
899		switch {
900		case exitCode != 0:
901			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
902		case resultRev == baseRev:
903			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges
904		default:
905			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
906		}
907	
908		// When the outcome is no_changes, attempt to read the agent's rationale file.
909		// The agent is instructed to write this file (relative to the worktree) when it
910		// determines the bead's work is already present. We read it before the deferred
911		// worktree cleanup removes the file.
912		if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges {
913			rationaleFile := filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel, "no_changes_rationale.txt")
914			if data, readErr := os.ReadFile(rationaleFile); readErr == nil {
915				res.NoChangesRationale = strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
916			}
917			if res.NoChangesRationale == "" {
918				res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence
919				res.Reason = "agent exited without a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt"
920				paths := dirtyWorktreePaths(wtPath)
921				res.NoEvidencePaths = paths
922				if len(paths) > 0 {
923					res.Error = fmt.Sprintf("%s; dirty paths: %s", res.Reason, strings.Join(paths, ", "))
924				} else {
925					res.Error = res.Reason
926				}
927			}
928		}
929	
930		// Classify failure mode from worker-level signals. ApplyLandingToResult
931		// may refine this with landing-level signals (merge conflict, gate
932		// failure) before the final result is output.
933		res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
934	
935		// Record routing evidence on the bead (best-effort; errors are discarded).
936		// Include requested passthrough constraints and power bounds so analytics
937		// can separate intent from resolved outcome (AC5 of ddx-20047dd5).
938		appendBeadRoutingEvidence(runtime.BeadEvents, beadID, resultHarness, resultProvider, resultModel, routeReason, routeBaseURL, rcfg.Passthrough(), rcfg.MinPower(), rcfg.MaxPower())
939	
940		// Record per-attempt cost evidence so cost rollup never has to join
941		// against the session index. Best-effort; errors are discarded.
942		appendBeadCostEvidence(runtime.BeadEvents, beadID, attemptID, costEventBody{
943			Harness:      resultHarness,
944			Provider:     resultProvider,
945			Model:        resultModel,
946			InputTokens:  inputTokens,
947			OutputTokens: outputTokens,
948			TotalTokens:  tokens,
949			CostUSD:      costUSD,
950			DurationMS:   res.DurationMS,
951			ExitCode:     exitCode,
952		})
953	
954		populateWorkerStatus(res)
955		if err := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res); err != nil {
956			return nil, fmt.Errorf("writing execute-bead result artifact: %w", err)
957		}
958	
959		// Optional out-of-band mirror of the bundle. Wired here so the whole
960		// per-attempt directory (manifest, prompt, result, usage, checks,
961		// embedded/) is on disk before the upload starts. Failures never affect
962		// the bead outcome — see executions_mirror.go.
963		MirrorOrLog(MirrorRequest{
964			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
965			AttemptID:   attemptID,
966			BeadID:      beadID,
967			BundleDir:   artifacts.DirAbs,
968			Cfg:         rcfg.MirrorConfig(),
969		})
970	
971		return res, nil
972	}

974	// dispatchAgentRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
975	// for the execute-bead worker. It threads the durable knobs from rcfg and the
976	// per-invocation plumbing from runtime through the shared dispatch seam in
977	// service_run.go.
978	//
979	// The script and virtual harnesses are DDx-side helpers that the agent service
980	// does not implement; the underlying RunViaServiceWith path delegates those to
981	// a private Runner internally, so they continue to work through this path.
982	func dispatchAgentRun(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
983		return dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, runtime)
984	}

986	// populateWorkerStatus fills in the Status and Detail fields on a worker result
987	// based on the task-level Outcome.
988	func populateWorkerStatus(res *ExecuteBeadResult) {
989		switch res.Outcome {
990		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
991			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
992		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
993			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
994		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
995			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
996		default:
997			res.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
998		}
999		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, "", res.Error)
1000	}

1002	// CommitTracker commits beads.jsonl if it has uncommitted changes.
1003	func CommitTracker(projectRoot string) error {
1004		trackerFile := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl")
1005		info, err := os.Stat(trackerFile)
1006		if err != nil {
1007			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
1008				return nil
1009			}
1010			return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker file: %w", err)
1011		}
1012		if info.IsDir() {
1013			return nil
1014		}
1015	
1016		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").Output()
1017		if err != nil || strings.TrimSpace(string(out)) != "true" {
1018			return nil
1019		}
1020	
1021		out, err = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "diff", "--", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").Output()
1022		if err != nil {
1023			return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker diff: %w", err)
1024		}
1025		if strings.TrimSpace(string(out)) == "" {
1026			out, err = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "ls-files", "--others", "--exclude-standard", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").Output()
1027			if err != nil {
1028				return fmt.Errorf("checking tracker untracked: %w", err)
1029			}
1030			if strings.TrimSpace(string(out)) == "" {
1031				return nil
1032			}
1033		}
1034	
1035		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: update tracker (execute-bead %s)", time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405"))
1036		commitOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
1037		if err != nil {
1038			return fmt.Errorf("staging tracker: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
1039		}
1040		commitOut, err = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "commit", "--no-verify", "-m", msg).CombinedOutput()
1041		if err != nil {
1042			return fmt.Errorf("committing tracker: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), err)
1043		}
1044		return nil
1045	}

1047	func resolveBase(gitOps GitOps, workDir, fromRev string) (string, error) {
1048		if fromRev == "" || fromRev == "HEAD" {
1049			rev, err := gitOps.HeadRev(workDir)
1050			if err != nil {
1051				return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving HEAD: %w", err)
1052			}
1053			return rev, nil
1054		}
1055		rev, err := gitOps.ResolveRev(workDir, fromRev)
1056		if err != nil {
1057			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving --from %q: %w", fromRev, err)
1058		}
1059		return rev, nil
1060	}

1062	func prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime) (*executeBeadArtifacts, error) {
1063		b, refs, err := loadBeadContext(wtPath, beadID)
1064		if err != nil {
1065			return nil, err
1066		}
1067		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(projectRoot, wtPath, attemptID)
1068		if err != nil {
1069			return nil, err
1070		}

1131	func ResolveGoverningRefs(root string, b *bead.Bead) []executeBeadGoverningRef {
1132		specIDRaw, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string)
1133		specIDRaw = strings.TrimSpace(specIDRaw)
1134		if specIDRaw == "" {
1135			return nil
1136		}
1137	
1138		// spec-id may be a comma-separated list of IDs or paths.
1139		ids := strings.Split(specIDRaw, ",")
1140		graph, _ := docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig(root)
1141	
1142		var refs []executeBeadGoverningRef
1143		for _, specID := range ids {
1144			specID = strings.TrimSpace(specID)
1145			if specID == "" {
1146				continue
1147			}
1148			if graph != nil {
1149				if doc, ok := graph.Documents[specID]; ok && doc != nil {
1150					refs = append(refs, executeBeadGoverningRef{
1151						ID:    doc.ID,
1152						Path:  filepath.ToSlash(strings.TrimPrefix(strings.TrimPrefix(doc.Path, root), string(filepath.Separator))),
1153						Title: doc.Title,
1154					})
1155					continue
1156				}
1157			}
1158			candidate := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(root, filepath.FromSlash(specID)))
1159			relCandidate, relErr := filepath.Rel(root, candidate)
1160			if relErr != nil || strings.HasPrefix(relCandidate, ".."+string(filepath.Separator)) || relCandidate == ".." {
1161				continue
1162			}
1163			info, statErr := os.Stat(candidate)
1164			if statErr != nil || info.IsDir() {
1165				continue
1166			}
1167			refs = append(refs, executeBeadGoverningRef{
1168				ID:   specID,
1169				Path: filepath.ToSlash(relCandidate),
1170			})
1171		}
1172		return refs
1173	}

1204	## How to work
1205	
1206	1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

1325	func buildPrompt(workDir string, b *bead.Bead, refs []executeBeadGoverningRef, artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, baseRev, promptOverride, harness string, contextBudget string) ([]byte, string, error) {
1326		if strings.TrimSpace(promptOverride) != "" {
1327			path := promptOverride
1328			if !filepath.IsAbs(path) {
1329				path = filepath.Join(workDir, path)
1330			}
1331			data, err := readPromptFileBounded(path)
1332			if err != nil {
1333				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("reading prompt override %q: %w", promptOverride, err)
1334			}
1335			return data, promptOverride, nil
1336		}
1337	
1338		var sb strings.Builder
1339		sb.WriteString("<execute-bead>\n")
1340	
1341		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=\"%s\">\n", xmlAttrEscape(b.ID))
1342		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
1343	
1344		desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description)
1345		if desc == "" {
1346			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
1347		} else {
1348			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", xmlEscape(desc))
1349		}
1350	
1351		acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance)
1352		if acc == "" {
1353			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
1354		} else {
1355			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", xmlEscape(acc))
1356		}
1357	
1358		// Bead notes carry review findings from prior iterations, escalation
1359		// context, or operator hints that did not fit into the description at
1360		// creation time. Threading them into the prompt as a distinct section
1361		// lets the agent act on them without the operator having to rewrite the
1362		// description in place on every reopen.
1363		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
1364			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", xmlEscape(notes))
1365		}
1366	
1367		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
1368			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", xmlEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
1369		} else {
1370			sb.WriteString("    <labels/>\n")
1371		}
1372	
1373		metaAttrs := make([]string, 0, 6)
1374		if b.Parent != "" {
1375			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("parent=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(b.Parent)))
1376		}
1377		if specID, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string); strings.TrimSpace(specID) != "" {
1378			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("spec-id=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(strings.TrimSpace(specID))))
1379		}
1380		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("base-rev=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(baseRev)))
1381		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("bundle=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(artifacts.DirRel)))
1382		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <metadata %s/>\n", strings.Join(metaAttrs, " "))
1383		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n")
1384	
1385		// For minimal budget, omit full governing refs and only include bead metadata.
1386		// This significantly reduces prompt size for cheap-tier attempts on local models.
1387		if contextBudget == "minimal" {
1388			sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n    <note>No governing references.</note>\n  </governing>\n")
1389		} else {
1390			if len(refs) == 0 {
1391				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <governing>\n    <note>%s</note>\n  </governing>\n", xmlEscape(executeBeadMissingGoverningText))
1392			} else {
1393				sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
1394				for _, ref := range refs {
1395					attrs := fmt.Sprintf("id=\"%s\" path=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(ref.ID), xmlAttrEscape(ref.Path))
1396					if strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title) == "" {
1397						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s/>\n", attrs)
1398					} else {
1399						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s>%s</ref>\n", attrs, xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title)))
1400					}
1401				}
1402				sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n")
1403			}
1404		}
1405	
1406		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(executeBeadInstructionsText(harness), "{{.AttemptDir}}", artifacts.DirRel)
1407		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", xmlEscape(instructions))
1408	
1409		sb.WriteString("</execute-bead>\n")
1410	
1411		return []byte(sb.String()), "synthesized", nil
1412	}

1438	// VerifyCleanWorktree checks that the project root's working tree has no
1439	// untracked execution evidence files for the given attempt. If evidence files
1440	// remain (e.g. because the land flow did not commit them), it stages and
1441	// commits them as a safety net. Returns nil when the evidence dir is clean
1442	// or was successfully committed.
1443	func VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, attemptID string) error {
1444		if attemptID == "" {
1445			return nil
1446		}
1447		evidenceDir := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID))
1448	
1449		out, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "status", "--porcelain", "--", evidenceDir).Output()
1450		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
1451			return nil
1452		}
1453	
1454		// Exclude embedded session logs from the evidence commit; they stay
1455		// on disk for post-hoc inspection but must NOT be tracked — the
1456		// multi-thousand-line .jsonl files are what caused ddx-39e27896
1457		// (retry prompts ballooning past provider context limits).
1458		// manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, usage.json remain tracked
1459		// per the existing audit-trail contract (gitignore un-ignores them).
1460		addArgs := append([]string{"add", "--", evidenceDir}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
1461		addOut, addErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, addArgs...).CombinedOutput()
1462		if addErr != nil {
1463			return fmt.Errorf("staging leftover evidence: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(addOut)), addErr)
1464		}
1465		diffOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", evidenceDir).Output()
1466		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) == 0 {
1467			return nil
1468		}
1469		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(attemptID))
1470		commitOut, commitErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
1471			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
1472			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
1473			"commit", "-m", msg,
1474		).CombinedOutput()
1475		if commitErr != nil {
1476			return fmt.Errorf("committing leftover evidence: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), commitErr)
1477		}
1478		return nil
1479	}

39	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
40	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
41	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
42	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
43	// captured.
44	//
45	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
46	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
47	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
48		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
49	
50		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
51		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
52	
53		var agent *AgentConfig
54		var workers *WorkersConfig
55		if c != nil {
56			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
57			workers = c.Workers
58		}
59	
60		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
61		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
62		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
63	
64		r.harness = overrides.Harness
65		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
66			r.harness = agent.Harness
67		}
68	
69		r.model = overrides.Model
70		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
71			r.model = agent.Model
72		}
73	
74		r.provider = overrides.Provider
75		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
76		r.profile = overrides.Profile
77		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
78		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
79		r.effort = overrides.Effort
80		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
81		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
82		r.passthrough = AgentPassthrough{
83			Harness:  r.harness,
84			Provider: r.provider,
85			Model:    r.model,
86		}
87	
88		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
89		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
90			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
91		}
92	
93		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
94			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
95		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
96			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
97		}
98	
99		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
100			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
101		}
102	
103		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
104	
105		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
106		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
107			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
108		}
109	
110		if agent != nil {
111			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
112			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
113				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
114				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
115					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
116				}
117			}
118		}
119		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
120			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
121		}
122	
123		return r
124	}

126	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
127	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
128	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
129	// ResolvedConfig.
130	//
131	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
132	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
133	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
134	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
135	//
136	// If projectRoot is empty, the process working directory is used.
137	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
138		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
139		if err != nil {

141		}
142		return cfg.Resolve(overrides), nil

data.text:
All hooks pass and the commit is clean. Here's what was delivered:

**AC1** — `TestExecuteOnService_MinMaxPowerReachServiceRequest` proves `ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower` and `MaxPower` carry the values from `rcfg` for every service-path invocation (covering `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`).

**AC2** — Pre-existing `TestExecuteOnService_PassthroughReachesServiceRequest` asserts Harness/Provider/Model come exclusively from the passthrough envelope.

**AC3** — `actual_power` added to `routingBody` in `appendBeadRoutingEvidence`; `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` captures it from `agentResult.ActualPower`; `TestAppendBeadRoutingEvidence_RecordsPassthroughConstraintsSeparately` now asserts it alongside `requested_min_power`, `requested_max_power`, `resolved_model`, and the passthrough fields.

**AC4** — `TestAppendBeadRoutingEvidence_ActualPowerReadableWithoutPassthrough` demonstrates that `actual_power` is a top-level numeric JSON field independent of the passthrough envelope — a retry policy can read it directly by unmarshaling the routing event body without inspecting any string fields.
